description: A conceptual illustration depicting the interaction between a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Platform, a Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) Platform, and a GNSS Station. The SAR Platform, represented by a satellite on the top left, is connected by a thin blue line to the GNSS Station on the ground. The GNSS Platform, represented by a satellite on the top right and explicitly labelled, is connected by a thick blue line to the same GNSS Station. A dashed black line connects a point on the thick blue line to a point on the thin blue line, forming a triangle with the GNSS Station, indicating a geometric relationship or measurement link between the two satellite signals as observed from the ground station. The illustration is labelled "(b)" in the bottom right corner.
